title: Elson.ai
description: A local-first macOS voice agent for dictation, cleanup, replies, and paste-first desktop work.
body:
Elson.ai is the voice layer I want on my own Mac: fast dictation, transcript cleanup, screenshot-aware replies, and a paste-first workflow that ends inside the app I am already using.

It is intentionally local-first and inspectable. Not another black-box assistant window. Elson sits closer to the desktop, listens when needed, cleans up the messy human input, and gives me text that is ready to use.

## What it does

- Turns speech into usable writing for chat, docs, email, and daily desktop work.
- Cleans rough transcripts into text that sounds like a person, not a recorder dump.
- Uses screenshot context for lightweight replies and better situational help.
- Keeps the flow paste-first so the assistant does not become the workspace.
- Treats the Mac as the real surface: permissions, app context, clipboard flow, and fast correction matter more than another chat tab.

## Recent work from local commits

The newest commits are very product-heavy. Elson gained persisted audio captures, menu copy buttons, chunked recording, recording-state refinements, lower-energy audio forwarding into ASR, screenshot-context optimization, post-response word correction, and refreshed distribution artifacts.

The 0.0.44 release work shipped modern and compatibility DMGs plus the universal installer. A later README pass sharpened the positioning around Elson as an open-source Wispr-style Mac voice assistant.

## What changed in the story

Elson is no longer only a voice demo. The public story is now a usable desktop utility loop: capture speech, preserve the right context, clean the transcript, correct the output, and make the result easy to paste or copy into the app where the work is happening.

## Public boundary

This page describes the public app surface, release artifacts, and product workflow. It does not expose private recordings, local user data, credentials, or unreleased account details.
